symbols.name: TimeProviderAccessor
symbols.summary: Provides ambient access to the current System.TimeProvider instance. This enables domain entities and other non-DI-aware code to access time without requiring constructor injection or method parameters.
symbols.remarks: The current System.TimeProvider is stored in an System.Threading.AsyncLocal%601 to ensure correct flow across asynchronous operations. In production, it defaults to System.TimeProvider.System. In tests, it can be overridden using TimeProviderAccessor.Current = fake.

symbols.name: Reset()
symbols.summary: Resets the current System.TimeProvider to null, causing BridgingIT.DevKit.Common.TimeProviderAccessor.Current to fall back to System.TimeProvider.System.
symbols.remarks: Useful in test teardown to avoid cross-test pollution.

symbols.name: SetCurrent(TimeProvider)
symbols.summary: Sets the current System.TimeProvider instance. Intended for internal use by ServiceCollectionExtensions.AddTimeProvider(IServiceCollection, TimeProvider).

parameters.description: The System.TimeProvider to set as current.
The System.TimeProvider to set as current.
